commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Needs contributor proof and a maintainer decision on the external registry lookup default before automation should repair or merge the branch.; Needs attention: The patch introduces a default-on outbound registry lookup path that can leak private dependency names or versions from review findings.; Review finding: [P1] Make registry verification opt-in before external lookups — src/config.ts:64 - [P2] Forward... (sha=5b68fdc3fe901230ddf77d852d949a6b2f1692f8)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a default-on npm registry post-validator for review findings, documents and configures a `registryVerifier.enabled` / `--no-registry-verify` opt-out, extends dropped-finding metadata and tests, and changes the build script to chmod `dist/cli.js`.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review findings by source inspection: the default config enables the verifier, `ciCommand` passes only `reviewFlagSubset` into `reviewCommand`, and that subset omits `noRegistryVerify`. I did not run build or tests to keep the checkout read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor proof and a maintainer decision on the external registry lookup default before automation should repair or merge the branch.

target.title: feat(review): drop registry-refutable findings from review output
target.body:
## Problem

LLM-backed providers with a fixed knowledge cutoff routinely surface "package X@Y is unpublished on npm" findings for versions released after the cutoff. The model can't know about post-cutoff releases without a tool, so the assertion is a hallucination dressed as ground truth. Scanning a ~7000-finding monorepo surfaced **88 findings** claiming packages that are in fact published (`mongodb@7.0.0`, `react@19.2.4`, `@types/node@24.10.4`, `vitest@4.0.16`, `@aws-sdk/client-kms@3.1000.0`, `tsx@4.21.0`, …).

## Approach

A post-validation pass resolves extracted `pkg@semver` specs against the npm per-version endpoint (`/{name}/{version}`). When a finding's nonexistence claim is refuted by a 200 with matching `name` **and** `version`, the finding is partitioned into `droppedFindings` with `layer: "registry-verifier"` instead of being surfaced.

Verdict matrix (biased toward keeping findings):

| Registry response | Verdict | Action |
|---|---|---|
| 200, matching name+version | verified-published | **drop** |
| 404 | verified-missing | keep |
| 200 non-JSON content-type | unknown | keep |
| 200 mismatched body | unknown | keep |
| 200 body > 1 MiB | unknown | keep |
| any redirect | unknown | keep |
| 5xx / transport / timeout / abort | unknown | keep |

Verifier failure never creates a false negative — only refutable claims drop.

## Hardening

- `redirect: "error"` so a 302 to an SSO gate reads as "unknown", not a drop
- Content-Type must contain `application/json`; body capped at 1 MiB via streaming reads (slow-trickle trips the cap before the wall-clock timeout; stream is cancelled, closing the connection)
- Response body `name` and `version` must both match the requested spec (defeats mirrors that answer any path)
- Per-request `AbortSignal` timeout with `.unref()` so the timer never delays process exit; caller signal combined via `AbortSignal.any` so Ctrl-C cancels in-flight calls
- Concurrent verifications of the same spec deduplicate onto one in-flight request

## Opt-out

Air-gapped or proxy-restricted environments: `--no-registry-verify` or `registryVerifier.enabled: false` in `.clawpatch/config.json`.

## Also included

- `fix(build)`: `chmod +x dist/cli.js` after tsc — the compiler doesn't preserve the exec bit, so the built CLI was emitted 0644 and direct execution failed with EACCES.

## Testing

40 unit tests: claim-pattern bank, spec extraction (scoped, prerelease, build metadata, sentence-boundary punctuation, email-shaped tokens), the full verdict matrix, mirror-trickery defenses, content-length cap, redirect rejection, in-flight dedup, caller-signal abort, non-Error throw resilience. `typecheck`, `lint`, `format:check`, and the full suite pass locally (one pre-existing `workflow.test.ts` failure on `main` too — caused by a global gitignore entry for `.clawpatch` on this machine, unrelated).
target.author: coletebou
